strategic_profile.executive_summary: Carnegie Mellon University is a global R1 research university distinguished by its intense interdisciplinary culture, fusing world-class computer science, engineering, and business programs with elite fine arts. Founded in 1900, CMU requires students to apply directly to one of its seven highly specialized undergraduate colleges, leading to highly variable selectivity based on the chosen major.
strategic_profile.market_position: Elite Private Research University / New Ivy
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic but heavily numbers-focused for STEM programs; highly portfolio-driven for Fine Arts.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Acceptance rates are deeply bifurcated by college.
analyst_takeaways.implication: An overall acceptance rate of 11% masks the reality that the School of Computer Science admits under 5%, acting more like a top-3 Ivy, while Dietrich College admits closer to 24%.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Testing policies are shifting strictly for the Class of 2030 (Fall 2026).
analyst_takeaways.implication: SCS will definitively require the SAT/ACT, while other colleges will move to 'test-flexible' (allowing AP/IB substitutions), ending the pandemic-era test-optional leniency for STEM.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Legacy and Demonstrated Interest are officially dead.
analyst_takeaways.implication: CMU does not track visits or open rates, nor does it factor in alumni relations, leveling the playing field but removing traditional 'hooks' for wealthy applicants.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A quirky, intense, and collaborative environment with strong Scottish traditions (bagpipes, kilts) and a heavy 'maker' culture highlighted by events like the annual buggy races.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in an urban but residential pocket of Pittsburgh (Oakland/Squirrel Hill), sharing a border with the University of Pittsburgh. Heavy reliance on public transit and walking.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Undergraduates have unprecedented access to research, particularly in AI, robotics, and machine learning, with CMU ranking #1 for start-ups created per research dollar.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for Fall 2025. For Fall 2026: School of Computer Science will REQUIRE SAT/ACT. College of Fine Arts remains test-optional. All other colleges (Engineering, Business, Science, Humanities) become 'test-flexible' (accepting AP/IB in lieu of SAT/ACT).

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admit rate is calculated based on students who accepted their spot on the waitlist.
Waitlist data for this cycle not publicly disclosed.
Waitlist admit rate sourced from historical aggregates.

gpa.notes: Nearly 47% of enrolled students have a perfect 4.0 unweighted GPA; 96.8% rank in the top tenth of their graduating class.

testing.policy_note: 52.6% submitted SAT; 22.4% submitted ACT. SCS heavily relies on the Math section as a filter.

colleges.name: School of Computer Science (SCS)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Only for top-tier math and computing students. An 800 SAT Math or 36 ACT Math is a soft prerequisite. Requires extensive technical depth, coding projects, or top STEM extracurriculars (e.g., USACO).
colleges.housing_profile: First-year students are required to live on campus (99.9% do).
colleges.student_archetype: The hyper-focused, mathematically gifted coder or AI enthusiast.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Requires a minor in a second subject to ensure depth in another area.

core_courses: 15-210 Parallel and Sequential Data Structures and Algorithms
15-251 Great Theoretical Ideas in Computer Science

majors.notes: Transferring internally from another CMU college is notoriously difficult. Requires a 3.6+ GPA in 6 specific core math/CS classes and passing a committee review.

colleges.name: College of Engineering (CIT)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For students wanting intensive, hands-on maker and builder culture with strong theoretical underpinnings.
colleges.housing_profile: Integrated with general university first-year housing.

majors.name: Electrical and Computer Engineering
majors.special_requirements: Four years of high school English, calculus, chemistry, and physics required.

core_courses: 18-100 Introduction to Electrical and Computer Engineering

colleges.name: Tepper School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking a highly quantitative, analytics-driven approach to business, differing heavily from traditional case-study management schools.
colleges.housing_profile: Integrated with general university first-year housing.
colleges.student_archetype: The analytical quant or data-driven manager.

core_courses: 70-207 Probability and Statistics for Business Applications
electives: 70-387 Strategy for High-Tech Products and Services

colleges.name: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students who excel in humanities but still want to study in a technically rigorous environment (e.g., Information Systems, Statistics & Machine Learning).
colleges.housing_profile: Integrated with general university first-year housing.

colleges.name: College of Fine Arts (CFA)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: World-class for Drama, Architecture, and Design. Acceptance is almost entirely driven by the audition or portfolio rather than test scores.
colleges.housing_profile: Integrated with general university first-year housing.

application_deadlines.notes: CMU does not offer an ED II option. Decisions released by Dec 15.
Some visual/performing arts programs may have earlier priority deadlines (Dec 1).

supplemental_requirements.details: Three short answers (300 words max each): 1) What passion/inspiration led you to choose your intended major? 2) How will you define a successful college experience? 3) Highlight something important to you or something you haven't had a chance to share.
Drama requires monologues/auditions; Design requires a digital portfolio.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: CMU strictly forbids supplemental resume submissions, maker portfolios (except CFA), or research abstracts to maintain an equitable playing field. Do not try to send extra documents.

major_selection_tactics: Target Dietrich College for a notably higher acceptance rate (~24%) if your profile is slightly weaker in STEM, but be aware internal transfer to SCS later is nearly impossible.
Apply Early Decision if CMU is your definitive first choice, as the admit rate jumps from ~11% to ~20.6%.
For STEM majors, do not apply 'test-optional' if you can avoid it; lack of a high Math SAT (780+) is a massive red flag for SCS and CIT.
college_ranking_tactics: Applicants can rank up to two colleges (e.g., SCS first choice, Dietrich second choice), but ED applicants can only apply to ONE college.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying to a secondary, less competitive college is common, but CMU rarely admits students to multiple colleges. They admit to the highest-choice college you qualify for.

financials.tuition_model: Flat rate for students taking 36 units or more per semester.
financials.aid_philosophy: 100% Need Met for U.S. Citizens/Permanent Residents. Need-aware for international students (who receive NO financial aid).

scholarships.name: Carnegie Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Historically awarded to middle-income students. Note: Official site states this and the Presidential Scholarship have been discontinued for incoming students since 2019-2020.

credit_policies.philosophy: Strict but college-dependent. Each undergraduate college sets its own AP/IB articulation rules.

ap_policy.general_rule: Generally grants credit for scores of 4 or 5.
exceptions: SCS and Engineering have strict limits on using APs for core requirements.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used for advanced placement or elective credits depending on the college.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants credit only for Higher Level (HL) exams with scores of 6 or 7.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer credit evaluated on a strictly individual, course-by-course basis requiring syllabi submissions.

what_it_takes: Flawless academic rigor in math/science (AP Calc BC, AP Physics C mandatory for STEM).
Demonstrated depth of technical or creative passion (e.g., GitHub portfolios, USACO, independent research).
Resilience and collaborative spirit, proven through essays.
essay_tips: Avoid writing generic 'Why CMU' praise. Focus tightly on specific academic alignment and future goals.
Use the third prompt ('Anything else?') to share a unique personal challenge or project update.
If waitlisted, send a concise LOCI detailing a newly completed project or skill learned, not just reiterating your love for the school.
red_flags: Low math test scores for STEM colleges (SCS, CIT, Tepper).
Submitting unauthorized supplemental portfolios or resumes.
Applying to SCS without calculus or advanced math.
insights: The 'Waitlist' is massive (over 16,000 offered in 2024) but operates essentially as a soft rejection, with admit rates commonly under 1%.
